title: Dhanendra Kumar: The Architect who gave Indian antitrust its teeth, and its heart
description: In the early days of the Competition Commission of India, hearings did not always look like hearings. There was no great courtroom theatre. No raised dais, no ritualised distance, no excessive formality. Lawyers, Commission members, officers and economists often sat around a conference table, working through facts, economics and law in a manner that was sometimes untidy, often searching, but almost always sincere.For a young competition lawyer, it was a wonderful room to be in. The law was new,
